body: Long before our time, the rooms of this house carried other lives. The "Weinstube" was once a relay station for the postal service, and later a set used in the filming of Jürg Jenatsch. The "Drei König" banquet hall has been, by turns, part of a monastery and part of the seat of government.

The first health insurance scheme for the forestry workers of Grisons was drafted under this roof. And somewhere in the building — depending on which corridor you take — sits what is recorded as the oldest preserved hotel room in Chur.

metaItems.label: Oldest hotel room
metaItems.value: The oldest preserved hotel room in Chur

imageAlt: The traditional "Weinstube" dining room at Hotel Drei Könige in Chur, featuring historic dark wood-panelled walls, an antique coffered ceiling with vintage chandeliers, and neatly set restaurant tables with white tablecloths.
